parking_lot_small.default: The lot is a slab of cracked asphalt and broken promises. You've worked worse.

time.morning: Dawn hasn't done the place any favours. Grease stains and cigarette butts are the morning crowd.
time.afternoon: Heat shimmers off the blacktop. Every car is an alibi waiting to be picked apart.
time.evening: The lot empties like a bar at last call — fast, and without a backward glance.
time.night: One lamp still burns. The rest are dead, same as half the leads you've followed here.

attribute.PERCEPTION: You clock every plate, every dent, every smear of mud that doesn't belong on a city car.
attribute.AGILITY: You cut across the lot the short way — ducking mirrors, skipping kerbs — because waiting is for suckers.

gender.male: You walk it like you own the block. Nobody checks a man who moves like he's got a reason.
gender.female: You case the exits before the door swings shut behind you. Old habit. Useful one.

parking_lot_large.default: A hundred cars and not one of them parked here by accident. Somewhere in this lot is the answer you need.

time.morning: Workers funnel in before the sun gets mean. Shift changes are the best time to watch without being watched.
time.afternoon: Full up and nowhere to hide. You keep moving — a still target is a dead one.
time.evening: The tide goes out. Engines turn over in the dark, and you watch which ones hesitate.
time.night: The lot sprawls under a bruised sky. One wrong shadow and this job gets complicated.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Three vehicles here don't fit the zip code. You've already memorised the plates.
attribute.STEALTH: A hundred places to disappear in a space this size. You pick the best three and rotate.
attribute.AGILITY: You move through the rows like a ghost — no sound, no straight lines, no witnesses.

gender.male: You stroll it like you're headed nowhere. The best tail never looks like one.
gender.female: You note every face that clocks you twice. In this business, being noticed isn't always a problem — if you control what they see.

parking_garage_small.default: Concrete and shadow. The kind of place where things get settled without paperwork.

time.morning: Early arrivals echo off the ceiling. You catalogue each one before they see you.
time.afternoon: Every level occupied. Every car a question. You don't leave until you have answers.
time.evening: The stream of departures covers your movement. Good. You need the noise.
time.night: The garage breathes on its own now — creaks, drips, the groan of settling steel. You breathe with it.

attribute.STEALTH: The pillars give you cover every twelve feet. You use all of them.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Sound bounces off concrete in ways most people never bother to learn. You learned long ago.
attribute.AGILITY: Every ramp, every chokepoint, every blind corner — you mapped it before you got out of the car.

gender.male: You stay in the shadows and let the dark work for you. The dark owes you at least that.
gender.female: You hit every stairwell light on the way up. Darkness is a tool — you decide who gets to use it.

parking_garage_large.default: Six levels of concrete and secrets. You've broken cases in worse places. Not many.

time.morning: The morning shift spirals upward in a slow parade of commuters who don't know what walks among them.
time.afternoon: Activity on every level. Good cover. Better hunting.
time.evening: Rush hour bleeds the garage dry. You wait for the last car and then get to work.
time.night: The exit signs throw red light on empty concrete. You know five ways out. You'll need at least two.

attribute.STEALTH: A structure this size has more blind spots than a crooked precinct. You've memorised them all.
attribute.PERCEPTION: You mapped the cameras on the way in. Three of them are fakes. Useful to know.
attribute.AGILITY: Stairs, not elevators. Always stairs. Elevators are traps with buttons.

gender.male: You move through it like you built the place — sure, deliberate, nobody's problem until you decide to be.
gender.female: You've learned to treat every multi-storey as a maze someone designed to disadvantage you. You solve it anyway.

transit_station.default: Noise, sweat, and ten thousand people who aren't watching each other carefully enough. Except you.

time.morning: The morning rush is a river of blank faces. One of them is lying to someone. Probably more than one.
time.afternoon: The midday lull. Easier to pick out the ones who don't have anywhere to be.
time.evening: Tired workers, running feet, no eye contact. The evening crush is where people think they're invisible.
time.night: The last trains scrape to a halt. Sparse crowd. Every face accountable. You like those odds.

attribute.PERCEPTION: You spot the one who doesn't move with the flow — stationary in a river of bodies. That's your person.
attribute.AGILITY: You thread through the crowd without breaking stride. Turnstiles, stairs, platforms — one clean line.
attribute.STAMINA: The noise and the press of strangers would grind most people down. You've been through worse and stayed standing.

gender.male: You pull your collar up and walk like you belong on the next train out. Nobody gives you a second look.
gender.female: You position yourself with your back to a pillar and your eyes on the exits. Same as always.

bus_depot.default: Diesel and dead ends. The bus depot smells like every city that ever chewed someone up and spat them out.

time.morning: Routes being called, engines idling, the whole machine grinding to life. Good time to lose yourself in the machinery.
time.afternoon: The rhythm of the depot is as predictable as a lie you've heard too many times. Which means it's hiding something.
time.evening: Last runs of the day. Drivers cutting corners. People in a hurry to be somewhere they shouldn't be.
time.night: The yard crew moves through the dark like they've done it a thousand times. Maybe tonight's the one that's different.

attribute.STAMINA: The fumes and the noise are the kind of thing that wears a person down. You stopped being that kind of person years ago.
attribute.STRENGTH: Big machines, heavy work, the kind of place where brute force passes for problem-solving. You respect the honesty.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Two buses moved recently. The logbook says they didn't. There's your thread.

gender.male: You blend into the yard crew with a nod and a look that says you've been here before. Maybe you have.
gender.female: You get the looks. You always get the looks. You use the thirty seconds of distraction to clock everything they're not watching.

convenience_store.default: Fluorescent light and stale coffee. The kind of place where lives quietly fall apart between the shelves.

time.morning: First customers of the day. The cashier looks like the night never ended. For them, maybe it didn't.
time.afternoon: The slow afternoon burns. Bored staff and restless regulars — someone in here is waiting for something.
time.evening: After-shift crowd. Tired, distracted, and telling the truth without knowing it.
time.night: Three a.m. and the place is lit up like a crime scene. One customer too many for this hour.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Security mirror angles, till placement, the back-room door left two inches open. You read the room in ten seconds flat.
attribute.INTUITION: Something's off with the guy by the cooler. He's been staring at the same six-pack for four minutes.

gender.male: You pick up a coffee and pay cash. Nobody remembers a man who buys coffee.
gender.female: You browse slow and talk less. The cashier fills the silence. People always do, and that's when they tell you what you need.

gas_station.default: Petrol and paranoia. Every gas station is a checkpoint — people pass through without thinking. You think for them.

time.morning: Commuters queuing at the pumps, barely awake. The perfect time to watch without being watched.
time.afternoon: Midday lull. The forecourt is exposed and quiet. Neither of those is necessarily a good thing.
time.evening: Evening traffic. Everyone's in a hurry to be somewhere. Find out where — that's the job.
time.night: The canopy lights blaze in the dark and nothing moves for minutes at a time. Then something does.

attribute.PERCEPTION: You count the cameras, note the angles, spot the car that's been idling at pump four for twelve minutes without buying anything.
attribute.AGILITY: You're back in the car before the receipt prints. Quick and clean, no loose ends.
attribute.INTUITION: That van's been parked on the air side since before you pulled in. It's not waiting for air.

gender.male: You pay at the pump, keep your head down. Transaction complete. Nobody noticed. That's the idea.
gender.female: The attendant offers help you don't need. You let them talk — chatty people fill silence with facts you can use.

fast_food_restaurant.default: Grease and noise and the steady misery of people who stopped expecting anything better. Your kind of place.

time.morning: The breakfast queue snakes to the door. Everyone's hungover or hasn't slept. Either way, their guard is down.
time.afternoon: Packed at noon. A thousand conversations happening at once. Pick the right one and it'll tell you everything.
time.evening: Families and teenagers occupy the booths, all of them watching their screens, none of them watching each other. Except you.
time.night: Night shift crew, hollow-eyed and running on spite. The real city, after-hours, laid bare.

attribute.CHARISMA: You smile at the counter and the server tells you more than the menu ever will.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Twelve tables, forty faces, and one person eating alone who arrived before the rush and hasn't touched their food.
attribute.STAMINA: The grease and the noise would sap most people. You chew, you watch, you stay sharp.

gender.male: You take the corner booth with your back to the wall. Old habit. Good one.
gender.female: You sit where you can see the door. The guy at the next table tries to make conversation. You let him think he succeeded.

coffee_shop.default: Seven-dollar coffee and secrets sold by the pound. Everyone in here is running from something or toward something worse.

time.morning: The morning rush brings them in loud and caffeinated. Loose mouths, tight schedules — money can't buy discretion.
time.afternoon: The afternoon crowd settles in with laptops and long drinks. They think they're hiding. They're not.
time.evening: Quieter now. The ones who remain have nowhere better to be. Neither do you.
time.night: Last customers of the night nursing cold cups. The barista wants to close. You don't care what the barista wants.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You pick a corner table with the full room in view and order something that gives you ninety minutes of cover.
attribute.MEMORY: The regular at the end of the bar — you know that face. Case file seventeen. The one you thought was cold.
attribute.CHARISMA: The barista has your order started before you reach the counter. Regular. Perfect cover.

gender.male: You open a newspaper you stopped reading three years ago. Nobody looks twice at a man with a newspaper.
gender.female: Two men near the window have been watching you since you sat down. You've already memorised their faces.

small_retail_store.default: Everything for sale, including information — though most shopkeepers don't know they're selling it.

time.morning: Freshly stocked shelves and a shopkeeper who hasn't had their guard up long enough to keep it steady.
time.afternoon: The slow burn of a quiet afternoon. Both you and the owner have time to size each other up.
time.evening: Closing time creeps in. People in a hurry say more than they mean to.
time.night: Shuttered. Whatever you needed here, you should have come earlier.

attribute.CHARISMA: The owner greets you like old money. You smile like you deserve it.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Mismatched inventory tags. A back shelf accessed too recently. Small things. You keep a list.
attribute.STEALTH: You browse the aisles like you're killing time. Nobody in here is being paid to watch you carefully.

gender.male: You ask a dumb question about a product you don't need. The owner talks for five minutes. Answers one question you actually wanted answered.
gender.female: The shopkeeper relaxes the moment you walk in. Threat assessment: none. Their mistake.

pharmacy.default: Prescriptions and secrets. People are never more honest than when they're sick and scared.

time.morning: Early customers collecting scripts they needed yesterday. Desperation has a particular walk.
time.afternoon: The lunchtime queue stretches down the aisle. A pharmacist under this kind of pressure makes small mistakes.
time.evening: Last calls before closing. The ones who come late don't want to be seen coming at all.
time.night: Dark and locked. Only the emergency window glows. Whatever brought someone out this late matters.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You read the shelves like a case file — what's low stock, what's over-ordered, what shouldn't be here at all.
attribute.EMPATHY: Every prescription tells a story. You're reading the chapters nobody came here to share.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The pharmacist's hands froze for half a second on one name. Just half a second. Enough.

gender.male: You linger by the vitamins long enough to catch the counter conversation. Health privacy ends where your case begins.
gender.female: The pharmacist answers your questions without hesitation. People underestimate how much women are trusted with the details.

bank_branch.default: Money is just a way of keeping score. You're here to find out who's been cheating.

time.morning: Doors just open. Nervous customers and tellers who haven't been lied to yet today. Give it time.
time.afternoon: Lunch-hour queue, harried staff, and the midday cover of noise. The branch does its dirtiest business in plain sight.
time.evening: Counting down the drawers. That's when the numbers stop lying and the people start.
time.night: Locked. The ATM hums in the dark. Someone's using it at this hour for a reason.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You read the floor plan in ten seconds — vault access, teller positions, the one camera with the bad angle.
attribute.INTIMIDATION: You walk past the guard without slowing. They straighten up. Good.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The customer three places ahead in the queue is sweating through a suit jacket on a cool day. That's not the weather.

gender.male: You carry yourself like someone who has an appointment. They always assume the appointment is real.
gender.female: The branch manager approaches you before you've taken a number. Women who walk in like they own the room get seen first.

laundromat.default: Everyone comes here with something to clean. Not all of it fits in a drum.

time.morning: The early crowd does their laundry before work. What they're washing out, that's the question.
time.afternoon: The machines drone on. Afternoon light through dirty glass. A good place to sit and think, if thinking is something you still do.
time.evening: After-work crowd. Tired, automatic, not watching what's around them. You are.
time.night: Two people and the sound of spinning cycles. At this hour, people are either working unusual shifts or they're hiding something. Sometimes both.

attribute.PERCEPTION: The man in the corner hasn't looked at his laundry once. He's watching the door.
attribute.MEMORY: That face — you know it from a different neighbourhood. Different context. Same trouble.
attribute.STEALTH: Fold something. Look occupied. A laundromat is the best surveillance post in the city.

gender.male: You sit with a magazine you're not reading and wait for the machine to give you something better to work with.
gender.female: You chat with the woman next to you. She tells you about her neighbour, her landlord, her troubles. One of those troubles is yours.

hair_salon.default: The most honest conversations in any city happen in a hair salon. People say everything when someone's working on their head.

time.morning: First appointments of the day. The stylists are fresh and the clients are loose-lipped.
time.afternoon: Full house, every chair occupied, and the noise level a cover for anything you want to hear.
time.evening: Last cuts of the day. The relaxed end of a long shift is when the real talk happens.
time.night: Dark and locked. Whatever conversation you needed, it happened six hours ago.

attribute.CHARISMA: You slip into the social rhythm of the place like you've been here every week for years.
attribute.EMPATHY: The chatter sounds like small talk. It isn't. You hear what's between the words.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Three conversations happening simultaneously. You're following all of them.

gender.male: You're the only man in the room and everyone knows it. The attention is useful while it lasts.
gender.female: You've been in enough salons to know that the mirror shows more than a reflection. You watch every face in it.

gym_fitness_center.default: Iron and sweat and the quiet desperation of people trying to outrun something. You know the feeling.

time.morning: The early crowd punishes themselves before dawn. Either very disciplined or very guilty.
time.afternoon: Midday regulars. Less competition, better sight lines. You watch the room like it owes you something.
time.evening: After-work rush. Every frustration in the city works itself out on a machine. Some frustrations don't belong in a gym.
time.night: The diehards. The ones who can't sleep or won't. You understand them better than you'd like.

attribute.STRENGTH: You rack the weights nobody else touches and feel the familiar burn. This is the one place the body doesn't lie to you.
attribute.STAMINA: You hit the cardio floor and settle into a pace that would wreck most people. You're just warming up.
attribute.AGILITY: You map the training floor in a circuit that looks like exercise and works like reconnaissance.

gender.male: Two regulars offer you a spot without being asked. Some spaces still run on old codes. You use it.
gender.female: You head for the free weights and ignore the stares. You've been ignoring them since day one. Doesn't stop you lifting.

strip_mall.default: A string of businesses held together by a parking lot and low expectations. Somebody's running something here that isn't on the signage.

time.morning: Half the shutters aren't up yet. The ones that are open early have a reason to be.
time.afternoon: Foot traffic and errands. The strip mall at its most normal, which means the least guarded.
time.evening: Most shops closing. The ones staying open after dark are the ones worth watching.
time.night: One light still on at the far end. You make a note of which unit and what time.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Twelve storefronts and one of them has inventory coming in after midnight. You file that away.
attribute.CHARISMA: You step into a shop and come out with three business cards and twice as many leads.
attribute.STEALTH: You walk the strip once and let the foot traffic swallow you. Nobody remembers a face that keeps moving.

gender.male: You move through it like a man with errands. Nothing says normal like a man with errands.
gender.female: You window shop. It's the oldest cover in the book and it still works because nobody ever questions it.

shopping_center.default: Consumption as cover. A thousand people who aren't watching where they're going, and one who is.

time.morning: The center's barely awake. Staff outnumber shoppers and everyone is running on autopilot.
time.afternoon: Peak hours. The crowd is the cover. You disappear into it without trying.
time.evening: Last rush before closing. People in a hurry make mistakes. You don't.
time.night: Security walks the dark concourse. You know their route before they've finished the first lap.

attribute.CHARISMA: You move through it like a welcome regular and people talk to you like one.
attribute.PERCEPTION: You've been scanning exits since you walked in. There's someone in here who doesn't want to be seen shopping.
attribute.STEALTH: You match your pace to the crowd and become part of the furniture.

gender.male: You keep moving. A stationary man in a shopping center draws attention. A moving one doesn't.
gender.female: You're invisible here in the best possible way. Just another shopper. Nobody's problem. Everybody's blind spot.

regional_mall.default: The regional mall: where ambition goes to die and surveillance footage goes to stack up. You've got work to do.

time.morning: Mall walkers in their circuit, the early stores cranking up. The lull before the storm is when you move.
time.afternoon: Packed. Every escalator a river of targets. You find the one who's swimming against the current.
time.evening: Last hour of trading. The security rotation thins and the pace picks up. Things happen in the last hour.
time.night: The big dark. Emergency lighting and a security patrol you could set your watch by.

attribute.CHARISMA: You start conversations by accident and finish them with what you came for.
attribute.PERCEPTION: In a crowd this size, finding the wrong person is easy once you know what wrong looks like.
attribute.STEALTH: This many people, this much noise. You could run a full surveillance operation from the food court and nobody would blink.

gender.male: You move with purpose through the browsing mass. Everyone assumes you know where you're going.
gender.female: In a place this size, a woman can become functionally invisible. You use it like the professional you are.

supermarket.default: Aisles and angles. Everything in a supermarket is designed to move you in one direction. You go your own way.

time.morning: Freshly stocked and barely staffed. The morning crowd is methodical. You blend right in.
time.afternoon: Busy aisles and distracted shoppers. Midday is when the delivery drivers come in the back. Worth noting.
time.evening: The after-work rush puts everyone in a hurry and nobody watching the edges.
time.night: Night-shift restocking in near-silence. The back corridors open up. Interesting.

attribute.PERCEPTION: You spot the gap on the shelf before the stockist does — and you know what it means.
attribute.MEMORY: Last time you were in a place like this, the prices told you a distribution chain was being bypassed. You're checking the numbers again.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Empty shelves aren't always out of stock. Sometimes they're evidence.

gender.male: You push a cart to blend in. Nobody questions a man with a cart. It's practically camouflage.
gender.female: The floor manager gives you a friendly nod. Women ask questions in supermarkets all the time. Nobody thinks twice.

warehouse_store.default: Everything sold in bulk, including whatever the back room doesn't advertise. You're here for the fine print.

time.morning: Quiet enough to hear your own thoughts and someone else's phone call two aisles over.
time.afternoon: Forklifts and noise. Good cover. You stay close to the walls.
time.evening: Thinning out. Easier to work but easier to be seen. Double-edged.
time.night: Closed. The loading bay out back is a different story.

attribute.STRENGTH: The scale of the place feels natural to you. You don't flinch at things that weigh as much as a bad decision.
attribute.STAMINA: It takes something to walk this floor without slowing down. You've got it.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One pallet's been moved recently but logged as untouched. You photograph the discrepancy.

gender.male: You look like you belong in the trade section. Nobody checks credentials when the posture is right.
gender.female: The floor rep walks over to assist. You ask three questions, each one designed to get the answer to a fourth.

restaurant_casual.default: Cheap meals and the kind of conversation that happens when people stop being careful. Pull up a chair.

time.morning: Eggs and alibis. Breakfast clients say what they mean because the day hasn't taught them caution yet.
time.afternoon: Lunch crowd, overworked and undertipped. Everyone wants to vent. You're a patient listener.
time.evening: Tables full of people saying things they'll regret later. Perfect.
time.night: The kitchen's shutting down. Last round of drinks, last round of honesty.

attribute.CHARISMA: The host gives you the good booth without being asked. You have that kind of face today.
attribute.EMPATHY: You read every table in thirty seconds. Two arguments brewing. One reconciliation that won't take.
attribute.PERCEPTION: You were seated three minutes ago and you've already mapped every entrance and the fire exit behind the kitchen.

gender.male: You order something cheap and make it last. A man nursing a coffee is invisible. That's the point.
gender.female: The waiter is attentive in the wrong way. You redirect the attention into useful conversation.

restaurant_fine_dining.default: Crystal and candlelight draped over the same old lies. The prices go up but the sins stay affordable.

time.morning: The restaurant is dark and silent. Only prep staff working the stock. They see more than the dining room crowd ever does.
time.afternoon: Quiet lunch service. Well-dressed regulars conducting business that belongs in a back room.
time.evening: Full house. Every table is a transaction. You're here to find out who's paying for what.
time.night: The last cover lingers. The staff wants to go home. Someone at table nine doesn't want to be overheard.

attribute.CHARISMA: The maitre d' decides in five seconds that you're someone worth accommodating. You give them reason to keep deciding that.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The wine list, the menu pricing, the clientele — this is a front for something. You're already three steps into finding out what.
attribute.EMPATHY: Table six has been smiling all evening. The body language says the conversation ended badly three courses ago.

gender.male: You order expensive and eat nothing. A man who orders expensive and doesn't eat is either nervous or important. Either works.
gender.female: Dining alone in a place like this marks you as either very confident or very dangerous. You let them wonder which.

car_dealership.default: Chrome and commission breath. Everyone in here is selling something. Some of them are selling cars.

time.morning: Salespeople reciting pitches before the floor opens. Pre-performance nerves. Good time to catch them off-script.
time.afternoon: Test drives and negotiation. Everybody's lying and everybody knows it.
time.evening: End of day, quota pressure, the desperation of people who need the numbers. They'll say anything.
time.night: Showroom lit up, no customers. The glass lets you see in without going in.

attribute.CHARISMA: The salesperson clocks your confidence and drops the canned pitch. Now you're having a real conversation.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You know the invoice price, the holdback, the incentives. The salesperson realises this at the same moment you tell them.
attribute.INTUITION: The floor manager keeps looking at the lot. Not at the cars — at one specific space. And what it used to hold.

gender.male: They open with the high end. You redirect to what you actually want. Takes thirty seconds.
gender.female: The salesperson underestimates you in the first ten seconds. You give them twenty more to dig the hole deeper before you climb out.

auto_repair_shop.default: Grease and graft. This place fixes things that are broken, which makes it a professional courtesy call.

time.morning: Engines turning over, diagnostics running. The day shift starts honest. Stays that way until the afternoon, usually.
time.afternoon: Bay full, every lift occupied. Good cover noise. You talk while they work.
time.evening: Last jobs being wrapped, overtime being argued. The day's secrets come out with the invoice.
time.night: The shutters are down. A light burns in the back office. That light doesn't belong to the books.

attribute.STRENGTH: The work here is honest and physical. You speak that language.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You pull up the diagnostic code they flagged and cross-reference it with a vehicle you didn't come here for.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One car in the back bay wasn't brought in for repairs. The plates were changed in the last twelve hours.

gender.male: You speak the shop floor dialect. They relax. That's when the real answers come out.
gender.female: The mechanic starts over-explaining. You let them. The explanation has a hole in it you can drive a case through.

medical_clinic.default: Sick people, tired doctors, and a filing system that knows everything about everyone. You just need one file.

time.morning: Waiting room filling up before nine. People who came early are either very ill or very motivated. You're the latter.
time.afternoon: Long afternoon queue, overworked staff, the kind of exhaustion that makes people careless with information.
time.evening: Last appointments running late. The clinic wants to close. You need two more minutes.
time.night: Dark and locked. Whatever you couldn't find out during hours, you'll need another way in.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You read the prescription pad on the counter, the dosages on the display shelf, the name blacked out on the file that shouldn't be on the desk.
attribute.EMPATHY: Every patient in the waiting room is scared of something. You can tell which ones are scared of the wrong thing.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The receptionist just hid a file that was sitting out when you walked in. She didn't think you saw. She was wrong.

gender.male: You give them a name that isn't yours and a reason that isn't real. The receptionist books you in without a second thought.
gender.female: You're through the door and past the reception line before anyone thinks to ask why.

dental_office.default: People say things in a dental chair they'd never say anywhere else. Pain is the world's oldest truth serum.

time.morning: First appointments wear the face of people who've been dreading this since yesterday. Excellent.
time.afternoon: The books have fallen behind. Waiting room patience running thin. Patience is something you have in bulk.
time.evening: Last patient shuffles out with a numb jaw and no filter. You catch them at the door.
time.night: Empty office, chairs tilted back in the dark. You've seen more unsettling rooms.

attribute.EMPATHY: You know what it costs a person to sit in that chair. You use the common ground.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The x-rays on the lightbox are interesting for reasons the dentist wouldn't appreciate.
attribute.PERCEPTION: There's a tension between the dentist and the hygienist that has nothing to do with a billing dispute.

gender.male: You take the waiting room chair nearest the corridor and read a magazine no one's opened since last year.
gender.female: The receptionist treats you like a person, not a case number. Some environments still work in your favour.

veterinary_clinic.default: Animals don't lie, and the people who love them are halfway honest. A good place to ask questions.

time.morning: First clients of the day — worried owners, anxious animals, and no reason yet to be on guard.
time.afternoon: Routine visits and the kind of chatter that happens when everyone's been waiting too long.
time.evening: Last few cases. The day's weight shows on the staff. Weight makes people talk.
time.night: Emergency hours only. Whatever brings someone here at this time of night — it matters.

attribute.EMPATHY: The clinic cat decides you're alright. You've been validated by worse sources.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The conversation in exam room two involves a word that doesn't belong in a routine check-up.
attribute.INTUITION: The owner waiting in the corner hasn't touched their phone in forty minutes. They're thinking. Hard.

gender.male: You hold the door for a client and get a full account of their last three visits in return. Politeness is an investment.
gender.female: The vet tech talks to you like a colleague. You learn more in four minutes than you would in an interrogation.

hospital_small.default: People come here to be fixed or to die. Either way, they tell the truth. You just need to be in the right room.

time.morning: Ward rounds and admissions, the machinery of care cranking into motion. Morning chaos covers your movement.
time.afternoon: Visiting hours. A tide of civilians moving through the building. You move with them.
time.evening: Families saying goodnight. Corridors thinning. The staff starts to notice faces that don't belong.
time.night: Night staff and emergencies. Quieter, more alert, harder to bluff. You do it anyway.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The chart rack tells you more than the patient's chart was supposed to.
attribute.EMPATHY: Everyone in here is carrying something beyond their diagnosis. You carry it with them long enough to learn what it is.
attribute.STAMINA: Hospitals never stop. Neither do you.

gender.male: You walk the ward with the posture of someone who belongs there. Nobody stops a man with a purpose and a pace.
gender.female: A nurse stops to check on you — genuine concern, no suspicion. You answer one question and ask three.

hospital_large.default: A city inside a building. Corridors that go on forever and secrets that go on longer.

time.morning: The complex hums with the controlled chaos of morning handovers. A new shift is the best time to walk in unquestioned.
time.afternoon: Every wing active, every corridor navigable. A building this size needs a good reason to stop anyone.
time.evening: Visiting hours bring civilians. You use the cover.
time.night: Skeleton crew, long corridors, and something in wing C that doesn't belong in a hospital.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You find the inefficiency in the system — the records room with the unmanned desk, the supply chain that doesn't add up.
attribute.EMPATHY: The weight of the place lands on you the moment you walk in. You carry it without breaking stride.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Restricted corridor. No badge. Moving quickly. You file all three facts and follow at a distance.

gender.male: You walk like a consultant late for rounds. Nobody questions a man walking fast in a hospital.
gender.female: You walk like a consultant late for rounds. It takes thirty more seconds to work, but it works.

urgent_care.default: Not sick enough for the ER, too hurt to wait for a GP. The people here are at their rawest. You use that.

time.morning: Yesterday's bad decisions are today's overnight walk-ins. Every one of them has a story.
time.afternoon: Walk-ins stack up through the afternoon. The triage desk is stretched. Stretched people cut corners.
time.evening: Day's end injuries and weekend starts. The evening rush brings its own variety of truth.
time.night: Night-time cases are a different category entirely. You've learned not to assume the obvious cause.

attribute.PERCEPTION: The injury doesn't match the explanation. You've heard cleaner lies from people who had more time to prepare.
attribute.STAMINA: The intensity of the place doesn't wear you down. It sharpens you.
attribute.EMPATHY: Behind every ice pack and waiting room grimace is a story that started somewhere else. You trace it back.

gender.male: You take the seat nearest the triage desk and look like a man with a pulled muscle. Nobody questions a pulled muscle.
gender.female: You're through intake fast — the staff here move quickly on presenting patients. You use the access before anyone second-guesses it.

daycare_center.default: Small humans, big noise, and staff trained to notice anything out of the ordinary. You are, by definition, out of the ordinary.

time.morning: Drop-off hour. Parents distracted, kids crying, staff stretched. Easiest time to slip in unscrutinised.
time.afternoon: Activities in session. The organised chaos is a kind of cover.
time.evening: Pick-up time. Parents running late tell you things they wouldn't say in a calmer moment.
time.night: Locked and dark. Any light here after hours is exactly as suspicious as it looks.

attribute.EMPATHY: You get down to eye level and the kid tells you exactly what they saw. Kids always do.
attribute.CHARISMA: The staff read you as harmless in seconds. People who work with children develop that instinct fast. You've learned to satisfy it.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One child has been watching the door since you arrived. Not waiting for a parent. Watching for something else.

gender.male: You sign the visitor sheet and wait for the staff escort. An unaccompanied man in a daycare gets the full procedure. You cooperate. It's faster.
gender.female: You're waved through the entrance with a smile. The assumption works in your favour. It won't last, so you move quickly.

elementary_school.default: Crayon drawings and the kind of secrets kids carry without knowing they're carrying them. Handle with care.

time.morning: Assembly echoes down the corridor. Every adult in the building is busy. The best time to be invisible.
time.afternoon: Classes in session. The halls are empty between periods — a window of maybe forty seconds.
time.evening: After-school activities. Fewer staff, looser protocols, kids who've been here long enough to drop their guard.
time.night: Locked and dark. A janitor's cart outside one room. The building keeps hours you don't.

attribute.EMPATHY: Kids read sincerity faster than any polygraph. You don't fake it — you mean it, which is why they talk.
attribute.CHARISMA: Teachers fall into step with you naturally. You carry the authority of someone who should be here.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The corner of one classroom is off. The usual visual noise of a primary school and then one thing that doesn't belong.

gender.male: Sign in, get the lanyard, accept the escort. The procedure slows you down but it's cleaner than the alternative.
gender.female: You move through the office with the easy manner of a visiting professional. Two teachers assume you're the new reading coordinator. You don't correct them.

high_school.default: Hierarchy, tension, and information passed in corridors at speeds most adults can't follow. You keep up.

time.morning: Social geometry re-establishing itself for another day. You read it in the first thirty seconds.
time.afternoon: Between-period surge. Forty seconds of controlled chaos in every corridor. Use it.
time.evening: Practice, rehearsals, detention. The building runs multiple operations after the bell. One of them concerns you.
time.night: Dark and locked. Whatever happened here today left its mark on tomorrow.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You read the social map of this building faster than the students who've lived in it for years.
attribute.CHARISMA: Students and staff both respond to you. You have the kind of presence that reads as official to anyone not looking closely.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The kid who should be in class isn't. You know the three most likely reasons. One of them involves the case.

gender.male: You get assessed immediately — visitor, authority, or threat. You settle into authority before anyone finishes the calculation.
gender.female: The front office waves you through. The second you mention a student's name, doors open that formal channels wouldn't.

community_college.default: Second chances and the quiet ambition of people who got a late start. Some of them are running from something. So are you, in a way.

time.morning: Morning lectures. Students who chose early slots are either very motivated or very employed. Worth knowing which.
time.afternoon: Study groups, common areas, the social glue of people who commute in and commute out. Good for a conversation.
time.evening: Evening classes. Older students, working adults, people fitting education around other lives. Less polished, more honest.
time.night: Campus quiet. Library still lit. Late students and the kind of silence that holds things.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The notice board tells you more than the admin office would. Who's recruiting, who's advertising, who's watching.
attribute.MEMORY: Academic environments run on paperwork. You remember every sheet you've glanced at, even sideways.
attribute.CHARISMA: Faculty and students both engage with you. You have the kind of curiosity people respond to.

gender.male: You're taken for faculty without trying. The assumption gives you range.
gender.female: Students ask you questions, assuming you're enrolled. You answer them and listen harder than they realise.

fire_station.default: These people run toward the thing everyone else runs from. You have more in common with them than you'd admit.

time.morning: Morning check is ritual — gear, vehicles, the discipline of people who know the cost of being unprepared.
time.afternoon: A call-out empties the station in forty seconds. You count the seconds and make your move.
time.evening: Evening shift settling in. The shared meal is where the real conversation happens.
time.night: Between calls, the station breathes. You're quiet enough to breathe with it.

attribute.STRENGTH: The crew gives you one look and moves over on the bench. You've passed the unspoken test.
attribute.STAMINA: Physical endurance is the baseline currency of this place. You have enough to get respect and enough left over to work.
attribute.AGILITY: You navigate the station's obstacle geometry without thinking. Old training. Doesn't leave.

gender.male: The crew reads you by your hands and your posture. They decide quickly. They decide right.
gender.female: The station goes quiet when you walk in — not hostile, just recalibrating. You let them recalibrate and get to work.

police_station.default: The precinct. Your old house, or someone else's old house, depending on the night. Either way you know where the bodies are buried.

time.morning: Morning briefing filters through the walls. The night's business being handed to the day. You listen for what doesn't get mentioned.
time.afternoon: Interview rooms occupied, case boards updated, the daily machinery of law and order grinding forward. You know how to slow it down.
time.evening: Day shift checking out, night shift checking in. The handover gap is the loosest point of the day.
time.night: Night watch. Fewer officers, lower guard, and the kind of quiet where you can actually think.

attribute.PERCEPTION: The case board has a gap where a photo should be. Nobody else noticed. You make a note.
attribute.INTIMIDATION: You walk in and the room recalibrates. Some people still carry that frequency. You're one of them.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The board connects three cases the detective handling them hasn't linked yet. You see it in thirty seconds.
attribute.CHARISMA: The desk sergeant gets you the file before you explain why you need it. Sometimes the old reputation still clears doors.

gender.male: Old badges don't expire in places like this. You walk in and the room assumes you still have one.
gender.female: Some of them still don't know how to read you. That confusion is a gap you walk right through.

post_office.default: Every letter is a conversation someone had to pay to have. You want to know what they said.

time.morning: First queue of the day. People who came to collect something that arrived overnight.
time.afternoon: Numbered tickets and long waits. The longer people wait, the more they talk.
time.evening: Counter down to one window. Whatever comes through at this hour, it wasn't sent through official channels.
time.night: Locked. The sorting room runs overnight. Shift workers who see everything and say nothing — until you give them a reason.

attribute.MEMORY: That face. That handwriting on the parcel at counter three. You've seen both before and not in this context.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The parcel addressed to a dead man is still in the collection bay. Third shelf from the bottom. You note the postmark.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Routing codes, postmarks, registered sender addresses. This building is full of traceable evidence and it's all technically public.

gender.male: You fill in a form you don't intend to submit and wait in line. A man waiting at the post office is the most unremarkable thing in the world.
gender.female: The clerk chats while they process. You listen. Postal workers hear things the internet never will.

library.default: Every answer you've ever needed was in a library somewhere. People just stopped knowing how to look.

time.morning: First readers of the day. The serious ones, the retired ones, the ones with nowhere else to go and nothing to hide.
time.afternoon: Students crowd the tables with open books and closed expressions. Research is another word for motive.
time.evening: The evening quiet is the library at its most honest. Fewer people, better conversations.
time.night: Closed. One light on in the back. Libraries keep their own hours when they think nobody's watching.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You move through the stacks with the precision of someone who remembers exactly what they read and exactly where it was wrong.
attribute.MEMORY: Call numbers. Layout. The periodicals archive that hasn't been indexed since the year of the Harmon case. You know exactly what you need.
attribute.PERCEPTION: A book pulled recently from a shelf that hasn't been touched in a decade. Someone's doing the same research you are.
attribute.INTUITION: The silence in here is a shade too deliberate. Someone's listening to you not making noise.

gender.male: You pull a reference book that has no business being relevant to anything current. The librarian doesn't ask. You appreciate that.
gender.female: The librarian offers cross-references. You take every one. Two of them lead somewhere the case file never thought to look.

community_center.default: Community on the surface. Underneath, every grievance, alliance and private arrangement the neighbourhood runs on.

time.morning: Yoga classes and coffee mornings. The social glue of a neighbourhood being maintained. And monitored.
time.afternoon: Committee meetings and after-school programmes. Everyone knows everyone's business and nobody says so out loud.
time.evening: The center is at its most alive. Events, classes, socials. The most people, the most noise, the most to overhear.
time.night: Last people locking up. The night's final conversation happens in the car park.

attribute.CHARISMA: You're three conversations in before you've reached the coffee urn. People like that you listen.
attribute.EMPATHY: The meeting upstairs is about a planning dispute. The one downstairs is about the same thing. Neither side knows the other is here.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The face you're looking for isn't on a committee but they're attending anyway. You clock them by the fire door.

gender.male: You show up, lend a hand, and earn a conversation. Community spaces reward the person who doesn't ask for too much too soon.
gender.female: You're welcomed in and handed a mug before you've introduced yourself. People here share things freely. You listen carefully.

city_hall.default: The machine that runs the city. Slow, rusted, and for sale to the right bidder. You want to know who bid.

time.morning: Officials running early meetings they'll spend the rest of the day denying they had.
time.afternoon: Council sessions and public consultations. The public face of decisions already made.
time.evening: The building empties. A few lights burn on floors they shouldn't. You write down the floor numbers.
time.night: Dark except for the security cameras. The real business of city hall happens in the dark.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Permit records, budget line items, zoning amendments buried on page forty-seven. The truth is always in the paperwork.
attribute.CHARISMA: You move through departments on the strength of a confident walk and a question that sounds routine.
attribute.INTIMIDATION: The deputy commissioner starts sweating when you ask about the contractor. Good. Let it sweat.

gender.male: You carry yourself like an auditor and nobody wants to talk to an auditor. They tell you everything to make you leave.
gender.female: A clerk offers to walk you through the filing system. You accept. The detour takes you past the office you actually needed.

courthouse.default: Justice — or the paperwork approximation of it. You've seen both up close. The paperwork lasts longer.

time.morning: Lawyers and defendants funnelling through the metal detectors before the docket opens. You watch who walks in together.
time.afternoon: Full session. Courtroom four has a case that keeps being adjourned. You want to know why.
time.evening: Proceedings winding down. Legal teams gathering in the corridor to say things they wouldn't say in front of a stenographer.
time.night: Locked. Duty officer at the front. The record room is on the third floor. You're not going to the record room tonight.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You read the body language of three witnesses across two cases and reach a conclusion none of the lawyers will reach until next month.
attribute.INTIMIDATION: Your presence in the gallery shifts something in the defendant's posture. That's information too.
attribute.PERCEPTION: A lawyer just passed a note they shouldn't have and received one they'll deny receiving. You saw both.

gender.male: You're read as counsel or investigator — either way, you belong here. Nobody's going to check.
gender.female: A female presence in the gallery reads as invisible to people who've been staring at the same faces all week. Perfect.

small_apartment_building.default: Thin walls and thinner excuses. Everything in a building like this gets heard eventually.

time.morning: Work clothes and rushed exits. The morning parade tells you who keeps regular hours and who doesn't.
time.afternoon: Quiet. The working day empties the building. Your window.
time.evening: Cooking smells and argument sounds drifting under doors. The building talks when it thinks you're not listening.
time.night: Still. The stairwell carries noise from three floors away. You use that.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Overflowing mailbox on 4B. Tape over the buzzer on 2A. A building narrates itself if you speak the language.
attribute.STEALTH: You take the stairs and press to the wall at each landing. No one sees you go up. No one sees you come down.
attribute.EMPATHY: The voices behind these doors tell you what kind of building this is. You've been in enough of them to know.

gender.male: You move through the building with the unremarkable confidence of a man who's supposed to be here. Nobody confirms it. Nobody denies it.
gender.female: A neighbour in the hall holds the door and starts a sentence about the management. You let her finish. It takes six minutes and gives you four leads.

medium_apartment_building.default: Layers of lives stacked on top of each other. What happens on one floor echoes on all of them.

time.morning: Lobby full of people who won't make eye contact. Morning in an apartment building is a masterclass in deliberate blindness.
time.afternoon: Quiet working hours. The building has time to breathe and so do you.
time.evening: The tide comes back in. Groceries, gym bags, the smell of every dinner in the city.
time.night: Floor by floor, the lights go out. One doesn't. You note the number.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Fresh scuff on the door of 7C. New lock on a door that had a different lock last week. The building is talking.
attribute.STEALTH: Stairs over elevator, wall-side over centre, steady pace over hurrying. Basic, but it works.
attribute.CHARISMA: The super comes out for a noise complaint on the same floor you needed access to. Convenient.

gender.male: You ride the elevator without eye contact. The universal signal for leave me alone. They do.
gender.female: The super is helpful in that particular way that means they want to be useful. You give them the chance.

large_apartment_building.default: A hundred apartments, three hundred lies, and one address that connects to everything. You just need to find which floor.

time.morning: The lobby revolves with departures. Morning anonymity at its peak.
time.afternoon: Deliveries and maintenance. Faces that belong and faces that don't. You know the difference.
time.evening: Returning residents and food deliveries provide enough traffic to cover your movement.
time.night: The overnight concierge has the desk. They've been there four hours. Their coffee is cold and their attention is colder.

attribute.PERCEPTION: A building this size runs on patterns. The broken one is on the fourteenth floor.
attribute.STEALTH: You time your movements to the delivery window. Three minutes of maximum distraction. More than enough.
attribute.CHARISMA: You carry yourself like a resident who's been here two years and you walk straight through.

gender.male: You nod at the concierge like a man who does this every night. They nod back. Transaction complete.
gender.female: The concierge is more attentive than you'd like. You give them a floor to call that gives you time on the one you actually need.

luxury_apartment_tower.default: Money spent on the illusion of safety. You're about to remind them that illusions don't hold.

time.morning: White-gloved morning service and a lobby so polished you can see the security team's reflection from fifty feet.
time.afternoon: Quiet, curated, watched. The afternoon hours here are the hardest to move through.
time.evening: Residents returning in cars worth more than most annual investigations. The lobby earns its marble at this hour.
time.night: The building purrs with the kind of quiet money buys. You're about to introduce some noise.

attribute.CHARISMA: You carry it off. The staff don't question people who look like they belong. You look like you belong.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Everything here is designed to look unblemished. The crack is always somewhere obvious once you stop being impressed.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The security protocol has three gaps. You identified them from the street before you walked in.

gender.male: You wear the building's assumptions like a coat. Nobody questions a man who walks in like he owns the lobby.
gender.female: The concierge's assessment takes two seconds longer than it should. You use those two seconds to clock the camera positions.

highrise_apartment_complex.default: Forty floors of private lives stacked in the sky. Everything you need is somewhere in the stack.

time.morning: Elevators packed, lobby a bottleneck. Everyone moving, nobody watching.
time.afternoon: The complex empties out. You have the corridors to yourself and a working theory to test.
time.evening: The building fills back up. You use the noise.
time.night: The security desk has one person on it. They've been here since eight. Their route is clockwork. So are you.

attribute.CHARISMA: You hold the elevator for a resident and end up with a conversation that covers three floors' worth of useful information.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Hundreds of units and one of them has a different air about it. You pin it to a number before you've reached the fourth floor.
attribute.STEALTH: Anonymous, crowded, and indifferent. Your natural habitat.

gender.male: You sign in under a name that matches someone in the directory and walk through like you've done it a thousand times.
gender.female: You control exactly what the front desk sees. They see a resident. That's all they need.

townhouse_row.default: Respectable facades with interesting interiors. Suburbs keep their secrets quieter than the city. Harder to crack. Worth the effort.

time.morning: School runs and commute starts. The street empties fast and stays empty. That suits you.
time.afternoon: Still and quiet. The kind of quiet that feels watched.
time.evening: Lights behind curtains, the smell of cooking, the sound of lives being lived. You read it like a text.
time.night: The row is dark and hushed. A dog barks once and then doesn't. Even the dogs know to keep quiet here.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Three lawns with full wheelie bins. One that's empty. One house that clearly hasn't been occupied this week.
attribute.EMPATHY: The neighbourhood has a tension under its orderly surface. You trace it to a house three doors down.
attribute.STEALTH: You walk the pavement at the pace of someone with a destination two streets over. You don't have one. Doesn't matter.

gender.male: You walk it once like you're cutting through. Second pass, you're gathering data.
gender.female: A resident watering their garden says hello. Neighbourhoods like this remember women they haven't seen before. You make the encounter count.

condominium_building.default: Owner-occupied, well-maintained, and under the impression that nothing bad happens here. You know better.

time.morning: The morning lobby rush is brief and efficient. People who own their apartments have somewhere to be.
time.afternoon: The building quiets after ten a.m. Easier to move, easier to notice anything that breaks the quiet.
time.evening: Returns from the workday. The concierge reads the evening crowd better than most. So do you.
time.night: Night-mode lobby. One desk lamp and a camera you've already catalogued.

attribute.CHARISMA: The concierge gives you the relaxed greeting they reserve for residents they've seen enough to trust. Useful.
attribute.PERCEPTION: You catch a reflection in the lobby mirror that places someone on a floor they haven't been assigned.
attribute.STEALTH: Shift timing. Blind-spot rotation. Entry window: ninety seconds. You've worked with less.

gender.male: You fold a newspaper under your arm and walk to the elevator. Man with a newspaper going up — nobody's problem.
gender.female: The concierge is chattier than their job requires. You trade small talk for building access and come out ahead.

senior_living_facility.default: The old and the overlooked. The city filed them away decades ago. That means they saw everything the city wanted forgotten.

time.morning: Routine runs deep here. Medications, meals, the quiet discipline of lives that have been lived long enough to know what matters.
time.afternoon: Visiting hours and activity sessions. The building is at its most porous. You use the traffic.
time.evening: Dinner in the common room. The most social hour of the facility's day. The most talkative.
time.night: Night staff, soft rounds, the hum of institutional care. And one resident who still keeps strange hours.

attribute.EMPATHY: You sit and you listen without looking at your watch. That's worth more in this building than a badge.
attribute.MEMORY: A resident mentions a name from forty years ago. You're the first person who recognised it. That changes the conversation.
attribute.CHARISMA: Staff and residents both take to you. You have the manner of someone who means it. They've earned the right to know the difference.

gender.male: You pull a chair up, ask nothing to start, and let the room come to you. It does.
gender.female: You're welcomed in with the particular warmth reserved for people who feel safe. You let them feel right about that and you get to work.

hotel_budget.default: Thin walls and cheaper regrets. Every budget hotel is a short story collection that nobody's proud of.

time.morning: The breakfast queue is a parade of people who slept badly and still have to function. You're among them. Sort of.
time.afternoon: The lobby is transit — everyone passing through, nobody staying.
time.evening: Check-in rush. One overworked clerk, a queue that tests patience. You use the wait to learn who else is checking in.
time.night: Somewhere down the hall a door slams. Somewhere else, a call ends badly. You lie on the bed and listen to the building tell its secrets.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Room 214 has been entered in the last hour. The latch is set wrong. Whoever's in there didn't grow up in hotels.
attribute.STEALTH: Indifferent staff and anonymous guests. This place was built for people who don't want to be found. That includes you.
attribute.MEMORY: You've stayed in exactly this kind of place before. Same layout, same broken ice machine, same smell. You know where everything is.

gender.male: You pay in cash and give the clerk a name that matches nothing in any system they'll bother to check.
gender.female: The night clerk is more attentive than the job requires. You smile until they stop being useful and then you go to your room.

hotel_business.default: Business travel: everyone with somewhere to be and something to hide. You fit right in.

time.morning: Laptop screens and phone calls at breakfast. The most productive lies of anyone's day happen here before nine.
time.afternoon: The lobby doubles as a meeting room for deals that aren't happening in the conference centre.
time.evening: The bar fills with people unwinding from the performance of being professional. Loosened ties, loosened tongues.
time.night: Corporate quiet. The hallways smell like carpet and confidentiality agreements.

attribute.CHARISMA: The front desk upgrades you before you ask. You have the look of someone who expects it.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The conference attendance list at the concierge desk tells you who's in town and why. Neither answer is what the signage claims.
attribute.PERCEPTION: You've clocked the room's sight lines, the corridor camera, and the stairwell exit before the porter finishes the room tour.

gender.male: You check in with the efficiency of a man who's done this a hundred times. You have. Just not always under the same name.
gender.female: You handle check-in in forty-five seconds flat. The clerk still adds an unrequested amenity. You accept it as access to a floor you needed.

hotel_luxury.default: Marble and misdirection. A luxury hotel is the best place in the city to conduct business that can't be traced.

time.morning: White-glove breakfast service. The conversation at table three started before you arrived and will cost someone dearly by noon.
time.afternoon: The hotel holds its breath in the afternoon. Everything about it says: nothing happens here. Everything about it is lying.
time.evening: Full dining room, full bar. Everyone dressed for someone else's benefit. You dress for cover.
time.night: The hotel turns to silk and shadows. The real conversations happen now.

attribute.CHARISMA: The concierge remembers your name before you've given it. You gave it three visits ago under a different identity.
attribute.EMPATHY: Every exchange here is a transaction. You read what's being bought and sold at each table.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The guest list, the room assignments, the kitchen supplier with the unmarked van — all three connect to the same name.

gender.male: You wear the hotel's assumptions like a second suit. Nobody questions the man at the corner table who hasn't ordered yet.
gender.female: A woman dining alone in the bar at this hour is either very confident or very dangerous. You're prepared to be both if the case requires it.

office_building_small.default: Small office, small secrets — but they're the kind that fit in a briefcase and travel well.

time.morning: Staff arriving, coffee in hand, guard not yet up. The freshest intelligence of the day is at nine a.m.
time.afternoon: Post-lunch slowdown. Everyone's a little more honest when they're a little more tired.
time.evening: Most of the floor dark by six. One office still lit. That office is the one you're interested in.
time.night: Empty. A single light on the top floor. That's not the cleaner.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Three minutes in and you've read the org chart, the room assignments, and the name on the door that doesn't appear on the chart.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Someone left a printout on the shared printer that shouldn't be shared. You read it on the way past.
attribute.STEALTH: Open-plan office, nobody watching the edges. You move like a consultant doing a walkthrough.

gender.male: You walk the floor like you own the contract. Assumptions run in your favour.
gender.female: You're past the reception desk before anyone thinks to ask who you're here to see. Purpose is its own credential.

office_building_medium.default: Mid-size offices, mid-size lies. The crimes here are the unglamorous kind — fraud, cover-ups, the daily corruption of small decisions.

time.morning: The building wakes with the particular energy of people about to spend eight hours being someone else.
time.afternoon: Conference rooms booked solid, hallways full of conversations that stop when you pass. You've already heard enough.
time.evening: The building drains by six. Whatever's still running at seven has a reason.
time.night: Security rounds and a cleaning crew. You know the gap between them.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The directory board tells you the official story. The gaps in it tell you the real one.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The elevator indicator shows floor twelve. Nobody has a meeting on floor twelve at this time of day.
attribute.EMPATHY: The staff on this floor are performing calm. You've worked enough interviews to know what performed calm costs.

gender.male: You carry the floor like you've been briefed on it. Nobody wants to be the one who stopped you.
gender.female: You walk it with the authority of the last person who was right. Nobody argues with the last person who was right.

office_building_large.default: A thousand people and one secret that ties all of them together. You're going to find it.

time.morning: The lobby revolves with the morning crush. Anonymity at its peak. You walk in with the tide.
time.afternoon: The building runs at full capacity. Noise, movement, and the comfortable cover of normalcy.
time.evening: The floor drains fast after five. The ones who stay are the ones worth watching.
time.night: Cleaners and security. Two schedules. One window between them.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: A building this size runs on hierarchy. You read the hierarchy in the email signatures on the unattended desk.
attribute.CHARISMA: Your visitor's badge should slow you down. Your manner says it won't.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The executive elevator shows recent activity for a floor that's been officially off-limits for a week.

gender.male: You carry the walk of a person with clearance and let the assumption do the work.
gender.female: You tailor the walk to match the floor and the floor opens up for you.

corporate_headquarters.default: The top floor of everything wrong with the city. This is where decisions get laundered and consequences get delegated.

time.morning: The executive floors have been active since before the sun cleared the skyline. The kind of decisions made at dawn are never the comfortable kind.
time.afternoon: Board presentations and media management. Every word calculated for an audience that includes you, whether they know it or not.
time.evening: Networking events and private dinners. The business of the day continues under different lighting.
time.night: The servers never sleep. The building holds its breath. You're inside it.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Power has a grammar. You speak it without accent. You also know exactly where the sentences fall apart.
attribute.CHARISMA: Senior executives open doors for people who look like they're about to open them anyway. You have that look.
attribute.INTIMIDATION: You make it clear — without raising your voice or breaking a smile — that you will not be managed.

gender.male: A boardroom full of men in suits assumes you're one of the men in suits. That assumption buys you twelve minutes.
gender.female: The room underestimates you and overestimates itself. You've corrected that equation before.

coworking_space.default: Shared desks and the illusion of community. Everyone working independently and desperately hoping someone notices.

time.morning: First ones in are claiming territory. Laptops open, headphones on, eyes everywhere.
time.afternoon: The space hits critical density. Pitches, calls, the low-grade performance of productivity.
time.evening: Product launches and meetups. The noise peaks and the guard drops. Your kind of hour.
time.night: Empty desks and dormant screens. Whatever was built here today will be dismantled somewhere else tomorrow.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: You're reading three pitches, two wireframes, and one conversation about a funding round that shouldn't be public yet.
attribute.CHARISMA: People introduce themselves to you before you've settled in. The social density of this place works in your favour.
attribute.PERCEPTION: Coworking spaces are porous by design. What's supposedly private here is only private to people who aren't paying attention.

gender.male: You take a hot desk near the back and open a laptop. Nobody here knows anyone well enough to notice a new face.
gender.female: Someone offers to introduce you around inside thirty seconds. You accept. Social capital accrues fast in places like this.

movie_theater.default: Dark rooms and people who came here to stop thinking. You came here to start.

time.morning: First screening of the day. Retirees and insomniacs who didn't go home. Honest company.
time.afternoon: Matinees for families who have no idea what you're doing in the back row. Better that way.
time.evening: Prime time. A hundred people looking at the same screen. None of them watching the exits. You are.
time.night: Late showing, sparse crowd, and a man two rows ahead who hasn't looked at the screen once.

attribute.PERCEPTION: You clocked the camera blind spot in the lobby before the doors opened. You know where not to be seen.
attribute.MEMORY: You've seen this film before. What you haven't seen before is the man sitting in the same seat as last time.
attribute.STEALTH: The dark is your advantage and you didn't need a ticket to remember that.

gender.male: You buy a ticket for a screen you're not sitting in and nobody connects the dots.
gender.female: You take the aisle seat. Old habit. The kind that keeps you alive.

bowling_alley.default: Pins and noise and the democratic misery of rented shoes. Everybody's equal in a bowling alley. That makes it useful.

time.morning: League morning. Regulars who've been showing up since before you were born. They've seen more than they admit.
time.afternoon: Families and birthday parties. Loud, distracted, good cover.
time.evening: The bar is doing real business now. People bowl worse when they're drinking and talk better.
time.night: Cosmic bowling. Black lights and music turned up past the point of caution. The night shift has different rules.

attribute.AGILITY: You don't throw like a civilian. The lane seven crowd goes quiet when you step up.
attribute.CHARISMA: You're offered a lane share before you've paid for your shoes. Social currency works fast in places like this.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The group at lane eleven hasn't bowled once. They're here to talk to someone. You want to know who.

gender.male: You accept the lane invite and spend two games finding out what you came here to know.
gender.female: You're underestimated at lane four. The score by the ninth frame corrects the record.

nightclub.default: Dark, loud, and built on the premise that nobody will remember what they did here. Somebody will. You.

time.morning: Doors locked, lights up, cleaners sweeping up the evidence of other people's bad decisions.
time.afternoon: The club is a dead room in daylight. Staff prep and the particular hangover of a place that only makes sense after midnight.
time.evening: Pre-opening. Sound checks and staff briefings. The performance hasn't started. Neither has the trouble.
time.night: The music lands before the door's fully open. This is the only time this place tells the truth.

attribute.CHARISMA: The VIP corridor opens without a conversation. You carry the right frequency for rooms like this.
attribute.STEALTH: You move through the crowd and leave no impression on anyone. Not because you blend in — because you choose to.
attribute.INTUITION: The energy in this room has an undercurrent the regular crowd hasn't caught yet. You feel it before you see it.

gender.male: The door staff step aside. The right kind of confidence reads in any lighting.
gender.female: Access is quick, which is its own kind of problem. You keep your back to something solid and your eyes moving.

sports_arena.default: Sixty thousand people and one of them knows where the body went. You're going to find out which one.

time.morning: Empty stands and groundskeepers. The arena stripped of its performance, just structure and maintenance. Easier to work.
time.afternoon: Pre-game activity building. The crowd filing in is the best place to find someone who doesn't want to be found.
time.evening: The main event. The noise, the energy, the cover of sixty thousand people not looking at you.
time.night: Post-game dispersal. The crowd thins and the arena resets. The people who stay after are the ones you want.

attribute.STRENGTH: You read the physical performance on the field with the eye of someone who understands exactly what it costs.
attribute.STAMINA: The crowd, the noise, the pressure — it wires most people up and wears them down. You run on it.
attribute.AGILITY: You move through a packed crowd the way a fish moves through water. Nobody notices. Nobody's supposed to.

gender.male: You're unremarkable in a crowd like this, which is the only thing you needed to be.
gender.female: You use the sight lines the crowd creates. Sixty thousand people looking one direction means nobody's watching the other.

warehouse_small.default: Loading bays and blind spots. Warehouses keep the city's secrets stacked on shelves and wrapped in plastic.

time.morning: Delivery vans and morning manifests. The paperwork that comes in with the first pallet is worth reading.
time.afternoon: Pick and pack, in and out. The rhythm of honest work covering whatever isn't.
time.evening: Last shipments dispatched. The dock crew wants to go home. Tired people make useful mistakes.
time.night: Locked up and alarmed. Except for the dock bay on the east side that was deliberately not alarmed.

attribute.STRENGTH: Heavy freight and high shelving — the physical language of this place. You speak it.
attribute.PERCEPTION: The inventory sheets don't match the shelf count. Someone's moving product through here without logging it.
attribute.STEALTH: Racking twelve feet high on every side. You could run an operation from in here and nobody would know where to look.

gender.male: You pick up a clipboard from the dispatch desk and look like you're checking something. You are.
gender.female: The shift supervisor does a double take and then gets helpful fast. You have your answers before they stop being helpful.

warehouse_large.default: Two hundred thousand square feet of things that aren't what they're labelled. Your kind of building.

time.morning: Forklifts and shift briefings. The morning's first movements reveal the overnight's real activity.
time.afternoon: Peak operation. Noise, machinery, the legitimate chaos that conceals the other kind.
time.evening: The day shift hands over with a thirty-minute window where nobody owns the floor. Your window.
time.night: Automated lines and skeleton crew. The building running on its own, accountable to nothing.

attribute.STRENGTH: The physical scale of this place earns a different kind of respect. You've earned it back.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One aisle hasn't been logged in three days but the shelf dust is fresh. Something was here and moved quickly.
attribute.STEALTH: A building this large, this loud, this busy. You are a footnote in the footnotes.

gender.male: You wear the high-vis hanging by the door and walk the aisles like you're doing a stock count.
gender.female: The floor manager appears fast. You have your questions ready. So does she. Your questions are better.

manufacturing_facility.default: The grind of machines making things the city uses without asking how. You're here to ask how.

time.morning: Day shift clocking in, machines warming up. The changeover is where the conversations live.
time.afternoon: Full production. The noise makes everything else inaudible. Good for a conversation you don't want overheard.
time.evening: Evening shift rotation. Two crews, two sets of information. You only need one.
time.night: Automated lines running without supervision. The humans are gone. Something else is using the building.

attribute.STRENGTH: Honest, physical work. You speak that language without condescension.
attribute.INTELLIGENCE: The production schedule, the supply invoices, the deviation logs — the machinery of this place is hiding something in the numbers.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One line is running out of calibration. It's been running that way for weeks. Someone told it to.

gender.male: The floor acknowledges you with the flat nod of people who assume you have a reason to be here. You do.
gender.female: The supervisor comes over to manage the situation. You let them manage it right into answering your questions.

data_center.default: The city's memory stored on racks in a cold room. Every secret that ever touched a network passed through a building like this.

time.morning: Day team running diagnostics on overnight alerts. Whatever the night shift caught, you want to know about it first.
time.afternoon: The hum of servers and the cold of precision climate control. The most honest room in the city: it never stops recording.
time.evening: Maintenance windows. Controlled access. Someone always has to be in the building during a maintenance window.
time.night: The facility runs without daylight. The servers don't sleep and neither do you.

attribute.INTELLIGENCE: Rack layout, cooling architecture, access log structure — you read the infrastructure like a floor plan of the investigation.
attribute.PERCEPTION: One rack is running three degrees warmer than its neighbours. That's not a cooling fault. That's activity.
attribute.STEALTH: You move with the certainty of someone with keycard access. Certainty is most of what keycard access buys you anyway.

gender.male: You badge through with the posture of an engineer who's been here a hundred times. The desk accepts the performance.
gender.female: The on-duty engineer is thorough. You answer each question accurately enough to pass and vaguely enough to stay mobile.

self_storage.default: Everything people can't throw away and won't admit to keeping. The private archaeology of a life. One of these units is yours to crack.

time.morning: Early access before the day gets complicated. People retrieving things they need urgently don't look casual.
time.afternoon: Regular traffic, regular faces — and one that doesn't belong to the pattern.
time.evening: Last entry before the after-hours lockdown. Whatever someone needs that urgently, it couldn't wait.
time.night: Access code required. Corridors empty and lit by motion sensors. The unit you need is at the far end.

attribute.PERCEPTION: Unit seventeen's padlock is a different brand from the one in the photo on file. Changed recently. Why.
attribute.STEALTH: The corridors are built for privacy. You use that against the person who thought they were using it for themselves.
attribute.MEMORY: You were here six months ago on a different case. The layout hasn't changed. The unit numbers you need are the same ones.

gender.male: The desk clerk hands over a unit key without questions. Men carrying boxes in a self-storage facility are the least suspicious thing in the world.
gender.female: The attendant is chatty. You learn the names of three long-term renters and the rough contents of their units before you've signed in.

church.default: Stone and silence and the weight of things people carry through these doors that have nothing to do with faith.

time.morning: Morning service. The devout and the guilty occupy the same pews without knowing it. Sometimes they're the same person.
time.afternoon: The church opens to the community. Confession, quiet reflection, and the odd parishioner who lingers too long near the vestry.
time.evening: Evening prayer. The smaller crowd means fewer faces to read, and the ones who show up at this hour mean it.
